highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.147.0 opens the v4 line on Skia m147 with variable fonts and animated WebP encoding.
highlights_body: The engine jumps from milestone 132 all the way to 147, bringing variable font support end-to-end (SkiaSharp and HarfBuzzSharp), color font palettes, animated WebP encoding, and new sampling options on surface draws. Tizen picks up x64/arm64 and Linux Bionic joins the supported native asset set.

breaking.title: GrVkYcbcrConversionInfo renamed to GRVkYcbcrConversionInfo
breaking.body: The Vulkan YCbCr conversion info struct now follows the SkiaSharp `GR` casing convention. Update any reference and rebuild — the old name and its IEquatable/Equals/== operators are gone.

breaking.title: Assembly version bumped to 4.x
breaking.body: SkiaSharp.dll now reports assembly version 4.147.0.0 (up from 3.119.0.0). Consumers with strong-name binding redirects or hard-coded version checks must be updated. Review the linked API-diff report before upgrading.

bullets.detail: The bundled engine advances through m132, m133 and lands on m147, picking up every upstream fix and feature between the two lines.

bullets.lead: Variable fonts and color font palettes
bullets.detail: Variable font support is wired through SkiaSharp and HarfBuzzSharp, and SKFont/SKTypeface gain color font palette selection and hardened variable-font handling.
Default typeface lookup moves to the managed layer, fixing #3693 and letting consumers observe/override the fallback without a native round-trip.

bullets.lead: Legacy font manager entry point restored
bullets.detail: The Skia shim regains `sk_fontmgr_legacy_create_typeface`, so downstream code depending on the legacy typeface factory works again.
New overloads accept SKSamplingOptions so callers can pick the mipmap and filter mode for surface-to-surface draws instead of the implicit default.
Turn any seekable stream into an SKData without an intermediate byte[] copy — useful when handing decoded assets to codecs and shaders.
SKWebpEncoder can now emit animated WebP files from a frame sequence, closing a long-standing gap next to the existing decoder.

bullets.lead: HBColor struct and missing HarfBuzz wrappers
bullets.detail: HarfBuzzSharp gets a proper `HBColor` struct (matching `hb_color_t`) and fills in wrappers that had been missing or mis-named — a step toward full API coverage.

bullets.lead: SKPath finalizer crash when SKPathBuilder collected first
bullets.detail: A GC ordering bug that could crash on finalization when an SKPathBuilder was collected before the SKPath it produced is fixed.

bullets.lead: Android SKGLView blank after MAUI TabBar switch
bullets.detail: Switching tabs in a MAUI TabBar no longer leaves SKGLView blank; the GL surface is now correctly re-attached after the view is re-added to the tree.

bullets.lead: Tizen picks up x64 and arm64 native builds
bullets.detail: Tizen ships alongside the existing x86/arm slices, so consumers on 64-bit Tizen devices no longer have to fall back to emulation.
Android-hosted Linux (`linux-bionic`) is a first-class native target — the runtime identifier now resolves to purpose-built binaries instead of the desktop-glibc build.
Libraries target the versioned Apple TFMs (26.0) while sample apps use the unversioned ones, matching current .NET SDK guidance and unblocking MAUI-style consumers.
Native builds for pre-.NET 8 Emscripten versions are removed — .NET 8 is the floor for the SkiaSharp WASM runtime asset.
libexpat 2.7.5, libpng 1.6.58, zlib 1.3.2.1-motley, freetype 2.14.3 and libjpeg-turbo 3.1.4.1 all pick up their latest upstream security and bug fixes.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: The Skia m132/m147 bumps, variable font support end-to-end, the managed default-typeface fix, and the SKPath/SKPathBuilder finalizer crash fix
contributor_summaries.4Darmygeometry: Linux Bionic native assets and PolySharp-based C# 13 support on legacy TFMs
contributor_summaries.SimonvBez: Fixed SKGLView not rendering after MAUI TabBar tab switches on Android

preview_summaries.p1: Preview 1 opened the v4 line: Skia m132 → m133 → m147, variable fonts, color font palettes, SKSamplingOptions on surface draws, the managed default-typeface fix, Tizen x64/arm64, Linux Bionic, and dependency refreshes for libpng/libexpat.
preview_summaries.p2: Preview 2 added animated WebP encoding, zero-copy `SKStream.GetData()`, the SKPath finalizer crash fix, and the Apple TFM realignment.
preview_summaries.p3: Preview 3 finished the API pass with HBColor and missing HarfBuzz wrappers, dropped native WASM builds for pre-.NET 8 Emscripten, and picked up freetype 2.14.3, zlib 1.3.2.1-motley and libjpeg-turbo 3.1.4.1.
